name: Jackson County 1St
description_short: Jackson County 1st Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union serving Jackson County, Florida with checking, savings, loans, and online banking services.
description_long:
Jackson County 1st Credit Union (NMLS #1905027) is a community-based credit union headquartered in Marianna, Florida, serving the Jackson County area. The institution operates with a focus on member-centered financial services and maintains traditional banking hours with both lobby and drive-thru access. Leadership includes CEO Valena Alexander and Chief Operations Officer Renee VanBuren, supported by a dedicated staff of loan and operations professionals.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking accounts, savings and investment options, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal loans, auto loans, and small business accounts. Members have access to online banking with bill pay capabilities, a TellerLine audio response system for account inquiries, MasterCard debit and ATM cards, and digital account management tools. The institution also provides calculators, check reordering services, and an online banking upgrade option for enhanced digital functionality.

Jackson County 1st distinguishes itself as a locally-rooted credit union with transparent fee structures and service fee information readily available on their website. They explicitly address tax law changes affecting borrowers, such as the 2025 "No Tax on Car Loan Interest" provision, helping members understand eligibility and calculate deductible interest from their annual account statements. The credit union maintains a published holiday schedule and clearly posted business hours to serve member convenience.

As a credit union, the institution operates on a cooperative model owned by its members rather than external shareholders. However, membership is limited to those within their defined field of membership in Jackson County. While they offer standard banking services comparable to regional banks, the website does not highlight competitive advantages in rates, rewards programs, or digital innovation beyond basic online banking and bill pay functionality. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Savings accounts and certificates of deposit (CDs)
Small business checking and lending accounts
Online banking with bill pay functionality
TellerLine audio response system for account information
Loan rate and service fee information
pros: Community-based credit union with local decision-making and member-focused service model
Accessible physical locations with extended drive-thru hours (9am-5pm weekdays) and traditional lobby service
Transparent fee structure with published Service Fees page available to prospective members
Online banking and bill pay capabilities for remote account management and payments
Small business accounts available alongside consumer products for local business support
Proactive member education on tax law changes (2025 car loan interest tax provisions)
Direct staff contact information provided for personalized financial assistance
cons: Limited to Jackson County membership field — not available to consumers in other regions
No information about interest rate competitiveness or APY comparisons with other institutions
Minimal digital innovation indicators — website shows basic functionality without mentions of mobile app, mobile deposit, or modern digital tools
Restricted hours on Tuesdays/Thursdays (8:30am-4pm) compared to Monday/Wednesday/Friday, limiting accessibility
404 errors on secondary pages suggest incomplete website content and potential navigation issues
best_for: Jackson County, Florida residents seeking community-based credit union membership with personal service
Small business owners in the Jackson County area needing business checking and lending relationships
Consumers preferring local credit union ownership structure over national bank corporate models
Members seeking straightforward banking without premium fees or complex product structures
diagnosis: Jackson County 1st Credit Union is best for residents within Jackson County, Florida seeking straightforward community banking with member-owned governance and local service. The primary limitation is geographic — membership is restricted to those within their field of membership area, making this unsuitable for consumers outside Jackson County seeking a credit union alternative.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
